claims.claim: Two-stage inference cascades reduce computation by routing high-confidence predictions through a cheap model and falling back to a full model otherwise.

evidence_refs.quote: # Partition-Local Confidence Cascade: Replacing Global Thresholds with Per-Partition Calibration in Two-Stage Inference Cascades

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: This work investigates whether replacing that global threshold with partition-local thresholds—calibrated separately within subgroups defined by the cheap model's predicted class and confidence bin—can increase the fraction of examples accepted by the cheap stage while preserving full-model accuracy.

evidence_refs.quote: # Partition-Local Confidence Cascade: Replacing Global Thresholds with Per-Partition Calibration in Two-Stage Inference Cascades

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: We evaluate on five datasets (one synthetic, four real) with five random seeds each, using logistic regression as the cheap model and random forest as the full model under a 1-percentage-point accuracy tolerance.

evidence_refs.quote: # Partition-Local Confidence Cascade: Replacing Global Thresholds with Per-Partition Calibration in Two-Stage Inference Cascades

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: On a synthetic dataset designed to exhibit partition-wise reliability heterogeneity, partition-local thresholds increased cheap-stage acceptance by 7.1 percentage points with a slight accuracy improvement (+0.23 pp).

claims.claim: However, across all 25 runs, the mean acceptance delta was −0.171, and partition-local thresholds won on acceptance in only 36% of runs.

claims.claim: A budgeted calibration variant achieved a higher acceptance win rate (48%) but exhibited calibration overfitting.

claims.claim: We conclude that partition-local confidence cascades are conditionally viable: they offer throughput gains when partition-wise reliability heterogeneity is present and calibration data per partition is sufficient, but they do not constitute a universal improvement over global thresholds.

evidence_refs.quote: # Partition-Local Confidence Cascade: Replacing Global Thresholds with Per-Partition Calibration in Two-Stage Inference Cascades

claims.claim: Inference cascades reduce computational cost by deploying a cheap model for "easy" predictions and invoking an expensive model only when the cheap model's confidence is insufficient.

evidence_refs.quote: # Partition-Local Confidence Cascade: Replacing Global Thresholds with Per-Partition Calibration in Two-Stage Inference Cascades

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: A logistic regression model may be highly reliable when it predicts class A with 90% confidence but unreliable when it predicts class B at the same confidence level, because different classes rely on different feature subsets with varying signal strength.

claims.claim: The hypothesis is that when cheap-model reliability varies across partitions, local thresholds can accept more examples from reliable partitions without violating an overall accuracy constraint.
